# Objectives

- Implement the automated ai video generation with Grok.
  - I will provide the Grok Imagine API key, you just place the placeholder API key name in `.env`.
  - The Grok Imagine video generation feature should be packaged in a single TypeScript class and used in every module.
- Implement automatic videos upload to Instagram.
  - I will provide the Instagram API key, you just place the placeholder API key name in `.env`.
  - The Instagram upload feature should be packaged in a single TypeScript class and used in every module.
- Implement the automatic video description generation for the Instagram with Gemini API.
  - I will provide the Gemini API key, you just place the placeholder API key name in `.env`.
  - The Gemini LLM feature should be packaged in a single TypeScript class and used in every module, so that when I pass this class method the prompt, it should return the response.

# Common Structure for the Instagram Accounts

- Each Instagram account has its corresponding folder in `src` folder, e.g. `daily-relaxation-rhythm`, `deep-relax-flow`, `the-revived-canvas`.
- Each Instagram account folder should expose simple API (functions), e.g. `generateVideos()`, `uploadOnIG()`.
  - Each execution of these functions should generate and upload batch of 20 videos.
- Each Instagram account folder contains two common folders: `videos-to-upload` and `videos-uploaded`. `videos-to-upload` folder is used to put the newly generated videos.`videos-uploaded` is used for the videos which are uploaded on the Instagram. The `the-revived-canvas` folder also contains `pics-todo` and `pics-used`. This is the only account that needs pictures (which I will provide), and based on these images it should generate the videos.
- Here is the prompt to use to generate videos for `daily-relaxation-rhythm`, with Gemini API:

  ```text
  Generate similar ai video generation prompt, but drastically different shapes, movement, still cosmos, but different kind of elements, different kinds or colors, many cosmic granular particles

  AI Prompt Samples:
  VARIATION 1 — TACHYON LATTICE STORM
  Concept: The hypothetical fabric of spacetime at the Planck scale — quantum foam rendered as a violent, beautiful granular storm of tachyon particles moving faster than light, visualized as overlapping geometric interference fields.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void object. Pure cosmic particle environment filling the entire frame in all directions with no empty center, no focal anchor, no horizon. The scene is a dense, turbulent, three-dimensionally layered storm of granular tachyon particles — hypothetical faster-than-light particles visualized as elongated streaks of intense luminosity rather than round points — each particle leaving a brief motion trail of two to three times its own length before fading. The particle population is enormous — tens of thousands of individual streak-particles simultaneously visible across all depth planes. The color system is internally inconsistent and exotic: particles in the nearest foreground plane burn in deep ultraviolet blue-white, their trails fading to pure violet. Mid-ground particles streak in intense chartreuse-yellow and acid lime, their trails cooling to deep forest green. Background particles glow in deep burnt sienna and rust orange, trails fading to dark maroon. The three color populations interpenetrate without mixing — a tricolor interference field of granular light. The movement directions are radically varied: some particle clusters move horizontally left to right across the frame, others diagonally at 45-degree angles, others appear to move directly toward or away from the camera — the full three-dimensional velocity space represented simultaneously. Occasional synchronized cluster events: groups of twenty to thirty particles of the same color briefly align into a thin geometric line before dispersing back into the general storm — a momentary crystal of order in the chaos, lasting two to three seconds before dissolving. The background between particle populations is not pure black but a very deep, barely luminous indigo-charcoal — the quantum vacuum itself faintly energized. No text. No UI. No figure. No void. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic granular particle storm simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A vast electromagnetic interference field rendered as audio — multiple overlapping frequency bands of soft broadband noise, each corresponding to one color population of particles, combined into a complex but unified ambient texture. The ultraviolet population contributes a high-frequency shimmer. The chartreuse population contributes a mid-frequency hiss. The sienna population contributes a low-frequency rumble. Together they form a dense but quiet three-layer cosmic static. Synchronized cluster events produce brief, soft tonal chord — present but not prominent. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion. Binaural essential. Loop-friendly.
  Style Tags tachyon streak particles with motion trails · tricolor interference field UV-blue/chartreuse/sienna · multi-directional velocity field · synchronized geometric cluster events · deep indigo-charcoal quantum vacuum background · enormous particle population density · no figure · no silhouette · no void · no focal center · full-frame particle fill · 9:16 vertical · seamless loop · granular cosmic storm

  VARIATION 2 — HELIOPAUSE MEMBRANE
  Concept: The boundary between a star's solar wind and interstellar space — the heliopause rendered as a shimmering, rippling granular membrane of colliding particle populations from two different cosmic regimes.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No central void. Pure cosmic particle environment. The scene represents the heliopause — the boundary membrane where solar wind particles from a star collide and interact with the incoming flow of interstellar medium particles. The frame is divided not by a hard line but by a complex, rippling, undulating boundary zone occupying the middle third of the frame — a turbulent mixing layer of extraordinary visual richness. On the left side of the frame, solar wind particles — fast-moving, hot, bright: intense gold-white, warm solar yellow, and deep amber — stream horizontally rightward in dense fast-moving sheets, their motion visibly energetic and directional, individual granular particles catching light like sparks in a forge. On the right side of the frame, interstellar medium particles — slower, cooler, denser: deep steel blue, cold grey-blue, and pale icy cyan — drift leftward in a slower, more diffuse counter-flow, their individual particles larger and dimmer than the solar wind particles, moving at roughly one-third the speed. In the central boundary zone, the two populations collide: their particles mix in a turbulent shear layer of extraordinary complexity — gold-white solar particles deflecting, losing speed, being captured into swirling vortex structures; steel-blue interstellar particles being heated and accelerated, brightening as they gain energy. The vortex structures in the mixing zone rotate slowly in alternating directions — adjacent vortices spinning opposite ways — creating a visual rhythm of rotating mixed-color particle clouds along the boundary. The boundary itself ripples and oscillates slowly — its position shifting left and right by 10 to 15 percent of the frame width over the loop, breathing with the pressure variations of the solar wind. Individual particles from both populations escape their flows and drift into the opposing regime — solar particles occasionally penetrating deep into the interstellar flow on the right, interstellar particles reaching the pure solar wind on the left. No text. No UI. No figure. No void. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic plasma boundary particle simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt Two distinct sonic populations colliding at the boundary — on the left, a warm, fast, high-energy hiss of solar wind plasma; on the right, a deeper, slower, cooler rumble of interstellar medium. In the center mixing zone, both sounds combine in a complex interference pattern that rises and falls with the boundary oscillation. Binaural stereo positioning places the solar wind sound left and the interstellar sound right, the mixing zone audible at center. Ultra soft overall. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion. Loop-friendly.
  Style Tags heliopause boundary membrane · solar wind gold-white yellow amber leftward flow · interstellar medium steel-blue grey-blue cyan rightward flow · central turbulent mixing zone · alternating vortex rotation structures · oscillating boundary position · particle population interpenetration · no figure · no silhouette · no void · no focal center · full-frame particle environment · 9:16 vertical · seamless loop · granular cosmic boundary

  VARIATION 3 — BARYONIC ACOUSTIC OSCILLATION
  Concept: The universe's largest sound wave — a pressure wave 500 million light-years across that structured the large-scale distribution of matter, rendered as a granular ring of cosmic density expanding outward in all directions simultaneously.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No central void. Pure cosmic particle environment filling the entire frame. The scene represents a Baryonic Acoustic Oscillation — the fossilized imprint of a primordial sound wave in the distribution of matter across the universe — rendered as a vast, slowly expanding granular ring of cosmic matter that fills the entire frame, its center already off-frame above, so only the lower arc of the ring is visible — a massive curved band of enhanced particle density sweeping slowly downward through the frame from upper regions toward lower, expanding outward. The ring itself is not a sharp edge but a broad, soft-edged enhancement zone — approximately 15 to 20 percent of the frame width — where particle density is noticeably higher than the surrounding field. Within the ring band: a rich, complex granular population — dark matter proxy particles in deep silver-grey forming the structural skeleton, baryonic matter in warm gold and pale yellow clustering along the dark matter framework, hot gas particles in deep pink-red filling the space between clusters, and young star formation particles in brilliant blue-white appearing at the densest nodes. Outside the ring — the cosmic void regions — far fewer particles, dimmer, more scattered: isolated grey-silver dark matter filaments connecting to the ring, occasional dim gold galaxy-analog clusters, vast stretches of near-emptiness. The expansion of the ring is extremely slow — the ring band moving perhaps 3 to 5 percent of the frame over the full loop, the cosmic scale of the motion making even this fraction feel immense. The background behind the ring is deep space: extremely faint galaxy smudges in all directions, a very subtle cosmic web filamentary structure in dim silver-grey connecting distant density nodes. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic large-scale structure particle simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A single, extraordinarily low-frequency resonance — the actual frequency of the baryonic acoustic oscillation translated into audible range — a note so low it sits at the very floor of human hearing, felt as vibration rather than tone. Sustained, pure, ancient. Above it, a barely audible mid-frequency harmonic shimmer representing the matter distribution along the ring. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion. The sound of the universe's largest confirmed sound wave made briefly audible. Binaural absolutely essential. Loop-friendly.
  Style Tags baryonic acoustic oscillation expanding ring · dark matter silver-grey structural skeleton · baryonic matter warm gold clustering · hot gas pink-red fill · blue-white star formation nodes · cosmic void low-density outer regions · dim galaxy-analog clusters · subtle cosmic web background · slow ring expansion motion · off-frame upper center origin · lower arc visible only · no figure · no silhouette · no void · 9:16 vertical · seamless loop · granular large-scale structure

  VARIATION 4 — PLASMA RECONNECTION CASCADE
  Concept: Magnetic reconnection events in a solar corona — the explosive release of stored magnetic energy as field lines break and reconnect, releasing enormous quantities of energetic plasma particles in chain-reaction cascades.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No central void. Pure cosmic particle environment. The entire frame is filled with a dense, dynamic, constantly evolving solar corona plasma environment — a vast field of granular particles in extraordinary motion organized by invisible magnetic field topology. The base particle population is a dense, hot, slowly churning plasma in deep orange-gold and warm amber — thousands of granular particles filling the frame from edge to edge, their motion slow and convective, rising and falling in broad thermal currents like an ocean of fire seen from within. Threading through this base plasma, luminous magnetic flux tube structures — bright white and electric gold — curve and arc across the frame in elegant geometric paths, each tube carrying a dense stream of fast-moving particles along its axis, the particles noticeably brighter and faster than the ambient plasma. At multiple locations distributed across the frame, magnetic reconnection events occur — moments where two oppositely directed flux tubes approach each other, their field lines touch, and explosive particle release occurs: a brief, brilliant flash of blue-white at the reconnection point, followed immediately by two jets of fast-moving particles shooting outward in opposite directions along the newly reconnected field line — a classic X-point geometry — the jets composed of extremely bright, fast-moving particles in electric blue-white and hot yellow-white, traveling rapidly outward from the reconnection point before decelerating and being absorbed back into the ambient plasma. These reconnection events are staggered randomly across the frame — never simultaneous, always one at a time, occurring every four to eight seconds at different positions — a cascade of individual energy release events distributed across the corona. Between events, the flux tubes slowly drift toward each other, building the next reconnection. Falling particles — plasma rain — descend along curved field lines from reconnection sites back to the lower corona in dense bright streams of warm gold. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic solar corona plasma simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A deep, warm, continuously churning base plasma resonance — the sound of an ocean of fire translated into low-mid frequency audio, present and full without being harsh. At each reconnection event, a brief soft energetic burst — a quick mid-to-high frequency pulse lasting less than one second, like a distant crack of electromagnetic energy immediately softened by the surrounding plasma. The reconnection jets contribute brief directional high-frequency sweeps in the binaural field. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion beyond the stochastic reconnection events. Warm and physically present. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 5 — DARK ENERGY EXPANSION FIELD
  Concept: Dark energy — the mysterious force accelerating the universe's expansion — rendered as a granular repulsive field pervading all of space, pushing particles apart at every scale simultaneously.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment. The entire frame is filled with a granular particle field representing dark energy's effect on the distribution and motion of cosmic matter — but the motion here is the opposite of gravity: everything moves outward, away from everything else, at every scale simultaneously. The particle population is vast and covers the entire frame with no empty regions. Deep background layer: thousands of tiny dim grey-silver galaxy-analog particles distributed across the entire frame, all moving very slowly outward from their neighbors — a uniform isotropic expansion so slow it is almost imperceptible, yet present and continuous. Mid-ground layer: medium-sized particle clusters — loose gravitational groups of ten to twenty particles each in warm amber and pale gold — their internal members moving slowly apart from each other despite their mutual gravitational attraction, dark energy winning at these scales — the clusters visibly, almost imperceptibly dissolving across the loop. Foreground layer: individual large granular particles in deep electric blue and pale violet — dark energy proxy particles themselves, representing the zero-point energy of the quantum vacuum — distributed sparsely across the frame, stationary in position but pulsing in brightness with a slow rhythm — bright, dim, bright, dim — as though the quantum vacuum is breathing, the energy density of empty space fluctuating at its characteristic frequency. Occasional void bubble events: a region of the mid-ground particle field spontaneously empties — its particles slowly pushed outward by dark energy into a growing spherical void bubble in the distribution, the particles accumulating in a thin shell around the growing void — a cosmic void forming in real time, slowly expanding until it reaches the frame edge. The overall color palette is deep space blue-black background, grey-silver distant galaxies, warm amber-gold mid-ground clusters, electric blue-violet foreground dark energy particles. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic dark energy expansion simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A vast, extremely soft repulsive resonance — the hypothetical sound of dark energy — a sub-bass tone that is not gravitational and pulling but somehow lighter, wider, spreading — a low frequency that seems to expand rather than compress the chest. The quantum vacuum breathing of the foreground particles produces a very slow, very gentle amplitude modulation in the overall drone — a cosmic breath cycle of approximately eight seconds. Void bubble expansion events contribute near-inaudible soft mid-frequency rings — a brief resonance as the void boundary passes. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion. Expanding and ancient. Binaural essential. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 6 — COSMIC RAY SHOWER
  Concept: Ultra-high-energy cosmic rays — particles accelerated to near light-speed by distant astrophysical sources — striking the upper atmosphere and cascading downward in vast particle shower trees of extraordinary complexity.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment viewed from above, looking downward through the upper atmosphere as cosmic ray primary particles arrive from deep space and cascade into particle shower trees. The frame is oriented vertically — space at the top, lower atmosphere at the bottom — and the primary cosmic ray particles arrive from above, moving downward. At the top of the frame: the incoming primary particles — ultra-high-energy protons and heavy nuclei — appear as single brilliant white points moving rapidly downward, arriving at staggered intervals, each leaving a brief intense trail. When each primary particle strikes an atmospheric nucleus at the upper-mid frame, an air shower begins: the primary splits into a cascade of secondary particles — pions, muons, electrons, photons — each secondary splitting again into tertiary particles — the cascade tree expanding downward and outward in a branching geometry of extraordinary visual complexity, like a lightning bolt tree growing downward through the frame. Each branching point is marked by a brief bright flash. The shower tree as a whole expands conically downward — narrowest at the primary collision point, widest at the lower frame where the shower front arrives. The secondary particles are color-coded by type: hadronic secondaries in deep electric blue, electromagnetic cascade particles in brilliant white and pale yellow, muonic particles in deep violet — their relative populations accurately representing the particle physics of real air showers. Multiple shower trees are simultaneously present at different stages of development — some just beginning at the top, some fully developed reaching the lower frame, some already dissipating — their branching structures overlapping and interfering. The background between showers: the deep blue-black of the upper atmosphere, with very faint auroral green wisps at the extreme top representing the interface with near-space. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic cosmic ray air shower simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A crisp, present, moderately soft cosmic ray ambient — each primary particle arrival produces a brief, very soft high-frequency click — present but never startling. Each air shower cascade contributes a brief, rapidly expanding broadband burst — a soft whoosh of particle multiplication lasting one to two seconds per shower, distributed across the binaural field with spatial positioning matching the shower's frame position. The continuous background of multiple overlapping showers creates a complex, ever-changing soft crackling texture — never loud, never rhythmic, always varied. Deep sub-bass atmosphere resonance beneath everything. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 7 — INTERSTELLAR DUST GRAIN CLOUD
  Concept: Extreme close-up at the scale of individual interstellar dust grains — the actual solid particles drifting between stars, each grain a complex world of ice mantles, carbon cores, and silicate structures, illuminated by starlight from all directions.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment at an intimate microscopic-to-mesoscopic scale — the frame represents a volume of deep interstellar space at the scale of individual dust grains, each particle in the scene an actual interstellar dust grain rather than a star or plasma blob. The individual particles are large enough in the frame to show internal structure — not simple points of light but distinct, irregular granular objects: roughly spherical to elongated, their surfaces showing the characteristic layered structure of real interstellar grains — a silicate or carbon core particle of deep grey-brown to dark rust, surrounded by a thin mantle of water ice rendered in translucent pale blue-white, outer surfaces showing patches of complex organic molecules in dark amber and deep maroon. Each grain is unique in size, shape, and composition — no two identical. The grains drift in slow, complex Brownian-influenced motion — individual thermal velocities randomized but with a very slow net drift in one direction representing the cloud's bulk motion through the interstellar medium. The illumination is the key: starlight arrives from multiple directions simultaneously — a warm gold source from the upper left representing a nearby K-type star, a blue-white source from the right representing a hot O-type star, a faint red source from below representing a distant M-dwarf — each grain casting multiple colored shadows and showing different surfaces illuminated in different colors simultaneously. Some grains are backlit — their ice mantles glowing translucent pale blue-white with the transmitted starlight. Some grains are front-lit — showing the warm gold silicate surface. Grain-grain collision events occur rarely — two grains drift close, touch, and either briefly stick before separating or bounce with a very slow elastic rebound. The depth of field is shallow — nearest grains are sharp and detailed, mid-ground grains are slightly soft, far background grains dissolve into a diffuse haze of tiny grey-brown points. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic interstellar dust grain simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt Near-absolute silence with the very softest possible ambient — the thermal radiation of individual dust grains translated into the lowest audible frequency, a barely perceptible warmth of sound rather than a tone. Occasional grain-grain collision events produce an almost inaudible soft low-frequency contact resonance — felt rather than heard. The multi-directional starlight illumination contributes three ultra-faint tonal layers — warm gold, blue-white, and red — at different frequency ranges, barely present. The overall texture is the quietest imaginable — the sound of matter at rest in deep space. Binaural essential. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 8 — GAMMA RAY BURST AFTERGLOW
  Concept: The afterglow phase of the most energetic explosion in the universe — hours after the initial gamma ray burst, the expanding shock wave illuminating surrounding interstellar material in a rapidly evolving multi-wavelength light show.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment. The scene represents the afterglow phase of a gamma ray burst — the initial explosion already past, its location off-frame above, and the expanding spherical shock wave now moving through surrounding interstellar material and becoming visible across the frame. The primary visual element is the shock wave front — a broad, luminous, curved band of compressed and heated interstellar material sweeping slowly downward through the frame from above as the spherical expansion brings it into view. The shock front itself is multi-layered in color from outer to inner edge: the outermost shock-compressed material glows in deep X-ray blue-white — the hottest, most compressed gas just behind the blast front; inside this, a band of optical afterglow in brilliant white and warm yellow; inside this, cooling gas in deep orange-gold; further inward, cooling further into deep red and maroon; and at the innermost visible edge, nearly cooled material in dim deep burgundy barely brighter than the background. The texture of the shock front is not smooth — it is Rayleigh-Taylor unstable, broken into hundreds of dense bright knots and finger-like protrusions where the shock is locally faster or slower, giving the front a richly detailed, turbulent inner structure. Behind the shock front — the swept-up interstellar material glowing in the afterglow's illumination — thousands of granular particles in warm amber and pale gold filling the frame in the shock's wake, slowly dimming over the loop as the afterglow energy dissipates. Ahead of the shock front — undisturbed interstellar medium in cold grey-blue and dim silver-grey, scattered and sparse. The background holds a richly detailed deep space field: distant stars as sharp points in blue-white and warm gold, a faint host galaxy haze in pale amber behind the shock site above. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic gamma ray burst afterglow simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A moderately present, complex multi-layer afterglow resonance — the shock front outer X-ray layer contributes a high-energy shimmer; the optical layer contributes a bright mid-frequency sustained tone; the cooling inner layers contribute progressively deeper bass frequencies — together forming a broadband descending chord that represents the cooling sequence of the shock. The overall sound is present and rich but never harsh — energetic without aggression. The Rayleigh-Taylor knots add a subtle broadband texture to the shock front audio. No melody. No rhythm. No percussion. Cosmically present. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 9 — QUANTUM CHROMODYNAMIC FOAM
  Concept: The strong nuclear force visualized at the quark-gluon scale — gluon field flux tubes connecting quarks in a seething, color-charged quantum foam of extraordinary density and color complexity.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment at the deepest possible scale — the interior of nuclear matter itself, where quarks and gluons create the fabric of all visible mass in the universe. The frame is entirely filled with a dense, rapidly evolving quantum chromodynamic field. The three color charges of quantum chromodynamics — designated red, green, and blue in the theory — are rendered literally as glowing granular particle populations in those actual colors: deep chromodynamic red particles, vivid QCD green particles, and electric QCD blue particles — each population representing one color charge of the quark-gluon plasma. The particles themselves are not static — they undergo constant color charge exchange: a red particle momentarily emits a gluon and becomes a green particle, the gluon carrying the color charge differential visually as a brief bright connecting thread between the exchanging particles before it is absorbed by a neighboring particle that changes color in response. These color-exchange events happen continuously and rapidly across the entire frame — thousands per second in reality, rendered at ultra-slow-motion as perhaps three to five visible exchange events per second per frame region — a constant, complex, beautiful dance of color charge redistribution. Between the quarks, gluon flux tubes — thin, brilliant white threads of intense field energy — connect nearby particles of appropriate color charges, forming a complex web of connections that is never static, always dissolving old connections and forming new ones as particles move. The overall density of the scene is extreme — this is the most particle-dense of all variations, with virtually no empty space anywhere in the frame. The background between particles glows with a very faint, dark chromodynamic field energy — not black but a deep, warm very dark maroon — the color of the QCD vacuum. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic QCD foam simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt The densest, most complex ambient of all variations — a rich, continuously evolving broadband resonance representing the QCD field energy — three color charge populations each contributing their frequency band: the red population contributes a warm mid-bass tone, the green population a mid-frequency shimmer, the blue population a bright high-mid resonance. Gluon exchange events add rapid, extremely brief micro-tonal clicks distributed densely across the binaural field — never individually prominent, collectively forming a subtle crackling texture within the main drone. The gluon flux tube web contributes a continuous bright high-frequency harmonic shimmer. Present and rich but soft overall. Binaural absolutely essential. Loop-friendly.

  VARIATION 10 — COSMIC STRING NETWORK
  Concept: Cosmic strings — hypothetical one-dimensional topological defects from the early universe — a network of infinitely thin, universe-spanning strings carrying enormous mass-energy per unit length, vibrating and intersecting across the frame in slow gravitational grandeur.
  Main Prompt No figure. No silhouette. No void. Pure cosmic particle environment. The scene is a cosmic string network — a web of ultra-thin, extremely massive one-dimensional topological defects stretching across the frame, each string glowing with its own characteristic emission and surrounded by a dense granular particle environment responding to its intense gravitational field. The strings themselves are rendered as extremely thin luminous lines — not particle streams but genuine geometric lines of near-zero width — each glowing in a distinct exotic color that is not found in any other variation: the first string burns in deep tachyon pink — a color that doesn't quite exist in normal physics, slightly beyond magenta; the second in hyperdimensional gold — warmer and richer than any normal gold, almost four-dimensional in its luminosity; the third in void-green — a deep, dark green with internal luminosity unlike any natural green. The strings are not straight — they vibrate in standing wave patterns, their shapes slowly evolving as they oscillate at relativistic speeds — the oscillation so slow in this visualization that individual loops and kinks drift and shift gradually across the loop. String intersection events — where two strings cross — are the visual highlights: at the intersection point, a brief brilliant flash of white light, followed immediately by either the strings passing through each other or exchanging partners in a reconnection event — two strings meeting, their ends exchanging, creating two new strings from the original two — a characteristic string network interaction. Around each string, its intense gravitational field organizes the surrounding granular particle environment: thousands of tiny cosmic matter particles — in deep silver, pale gold, and dim blue-white — orbit the strings in loose gravitational captures, denser near the string, diffusing outward. Between strings: cosmic void regions — nearly empty of particles but not quite, the sparse interstring medium of dim grey particles slowly drifting. The background behind everything: a deep space environment of extreme darkness, with very faint cosmic web filamentary structure barely visible in dim silver-grey, and a scattering of distant galaxy-analog points in pale amber and cold blue-white. No text. No UI. No figure. Cinematic 9:16 vertical format, 4K, photorealistic cosmic string network simulation, seamless loop.
  Sound / Audio Prompt A vast, exotic, multi-tonal cosmic string resonance — each string vibrates at a characteristic frequency determined by its tension and length, translated into audio: the tachyon-pink string contributes a deep, strange low-frequency drone with slight inharmonic overtones; the hyperdimensional-gold string contributes a warm, rich mid-frequency sustained tone; the void-green string contributes a cool, hollow high-mid resonance. Together they form a cosmic string chord — three notes that don't quite belong together but create a haunting, unresolved harmonic tension. String intersection events produce brief, soft white-noise bursts — a flash of broadband energy at the reconnection moment. The surrounding particle fields contribute a continuous gentle ambient shimmer. No melody. No percussion. No rhythm beyond the stochastic intersection events. Strange and beautiful. Binaural absolutely essential. Loop-friendly.
  ```

- Here is the prompt to use to generate videos for `deep-relax-flow`, with Gemini API:

  ```text
  Generate similar AI video generation prompt:
  AI Prompt Sample
  1. Molten Velvet Morphing
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A macro cinematic study of deep emerald green and mustard yellow velvet geometries—spheres, toruses, and polyhedrons—slowly losing their structural integrity. The fabric texture is hyper-realistic, showing fine fibers and a soft sheen. As the shapes collide, they don't shatter but melt into one another like thick, viscous mercury. Occasional splashes of vibrant magenta and cobalt blue emerge from the center of the melting shapes, creating a rich, multi-textured fusion. The lighting is soft and diffused, catching the "fuzz" of the velvet as it liquefies.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  A low-frequency, muffled textural soundscape. Soft, rhythmic "thumps" and the sound of heavy fabric sliding against itself. Faint, airy whooshes as the shapes reshape, devoid of any sharp or metallic edges. A deep, grounding brown noise floor that feels like being wrapped in a heavy blanket.

  2. Kinetic Plastic Heat-Mapping
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Translucent matte plastic forms in lime green and lemon yellow begin to soften and sag under an unseen heat source. The shapes stretch and pull like warm taffy, creating thin, vein-like filaments that connect drifting masses. The color palette transitions where the shapes stretch, revealing internal layers of neon orange and soft teal. The movement is glacial and constant. The background is a neutral, warm grey, making the high-saturation plastic "pop."
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  ASMR-inspired tactile sounds. The gentle, slow-motion stretching of latex or soft vinyl. Sub-bass pulses that sync with the expansion of the plastic. No melodic elements; only the organic, squelching sound of pliable materials shifting in a pressurized environment.

  3. The Polymer Tide
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A top-down view of a dense field of solid, sage-green cubes and golden-yellow spheres. A "wave" of heat passes over the frame, causing the solids to instantly liquefy into a unified, swirling river of color. The liquid has the consistency of heavy cream or thick paint. Small, bead-like droplets of lilac and crimson float on the surface, resisting the mix. The shapes eventually reform into new, abstract organic mounds before melting again in a seamless loop.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Thick, liquid movement. The sound of a heavy viscous fluid bubbling very slowly—large, soft bubbles popping with a deep "glup" sound. A soft, white-noise hiss resembling a distant steamer, providing a sense of warmth and humidity.

  4. Satin-Finished Blobular Synthesis
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A cluster of floating, amorphous blobs with a "soft-touch" plastic finish in olive and canary yellow. The blobs move with a magnetic attraction, merging upon contact into larger, complex organisms. When they merge, a brief flash of iridescent pearl white ripples across their surface. The "other" colors—burnt orange and deep violet—act as "cores" inside the translucent green and yellow shells. The camera moves in a slow, 360-degree orbit around the central mass.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Ethereal, resonant tones. Soft, tonal "bloops" that occur when two shapes merge, followed by a long, decaying reverb. The sound of a finger sliding over a smooth balloon. A shimmering, high-frequency "crystal" texture that remains at a very low volume in the background.

  5. Pliable Geometries in Flux
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A series of interlocking green and yellow plastic rings that slowly soften until they resemble rings of liquid light. As the material "melts," it forms intricate, lace-like patterns before collapsing into a single, rotating sphere of multicolored marbled wax. The colors (green, yellow, and hits of electric pink) do not mix fully but remain in distinct, beautiful ribbons. The surface remains matte and non-reflective, emphasizing the purity of the pigment.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  A minimalist "crinkle" and "stretch" soundscape. The sound of soft wax being kneaded by hand. A low-pass filtered ambient drone that ebbs and flows with the size of the shapes. Completely relaxing, with a focus on the "weight" of the sound to match the density of the material.
  6. Sculptural Silicone Symbiosis
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A collection of heavy, matte silicone cylinders in forest green and sunset yellow standing on a vibrating plane. As the frequency increases, the solid cylinders begin to "weep" and liquefy from the top down, turning into a puddling, gelatinous mass. Secondary colors of terracotta and dusty rose appear as thin "marbling" inside the green and yellow flows. The material has a high surface tension, forming large, wobbling droplets that merge and pull apart like magnetic slime. The environment is dark and moody, with a single soft spotlight highlighting the velvety texture of the silicone.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Deep, resonant vibrations. The sound of a large tuning fork vibrating through water. Thick, "wet" suction sounds as the silicone droplets pull apart and reform. A steady, low-frequency hum that mimics the vibration seen on screen, creating a tactile audio-visual experience.

  7. The Over-Saturated Wax Bloom
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A macro view of a "bloom" of solid wax shapes—petals, ridges, and spines—in brilliant lime green and neon yellow. A localized heat source causes the sharp edges to round off and slump into soft, pillowy mounds. As the wax liquefies, hidden "pigment pockets" of deep cyan and fluorescent orange burst and bleed into the main stream. The liquid is opaque and dense, resembling melted crayons. The camera slowly zooms into the center of a merging puddle, losing all sense of scale.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  The gentle, rhythmic sound of a slow-dripping faucet into a thick liquid. Soft, waxy "clicks" and the sound of a slow, warm breeze. A background of "pink noise" that feels like the interior of a seashell, providing a constant, soothing atmosphere.

  8. Floating Polymer Plasma
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Zero-gravity blobs of "soft-touch" plastic in pearlescent green and lemon yellow. The blobs are semi-solid, behaving like a hybrid between a stress ball and a lava lamp. When two blobs collide, they flatten against each other before slowly absorbing into a single unit, changing color to a soft, blended chartreuse. Specks of metallic gold and deep navy blue float within the blobs like glitter in suspension. The movement is effortless and airy, with the camera drifting alongside the shapes.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Granular, sparkling textures. The sound of thousands of tiny beads rolling over silk. Very soft, high-pitched "pings" that resonate like distant wind chimes. A feeling of "breath" in the audio, with slow, deep swells of volume that align with the shapes expanding and contracting.

  9. The Velvet Tectonics
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Large, interlocking slabs of moss-green and honey-yellow velvet. The slabs move like tectonic plates, grinding against each other. Instead of crumbling, the edges turn into a thick, glowing liquid that fills the "valleys" between the plates. This liquid is a contrasting violet and silver. The transition from the fuzzy, light-absorbing velvet to the glowing, light-emitting liquid is the focal point. The lighting shifts from cool to warm as the material changes state.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Textural friction. The sound of a heavy brush moving across a canvas. A deep, subterranean "groan" that is smoothed out by the sound of pouring cream. No sudden movements; the audio is a continuous, evolving drone that feels physically heavy yet emotionally calming.

  10. Bioluminescent Dough Extrusions
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Organic, dough-like shapes in seafoam green and pale yellow being squeezed through invisible apertures. The "dough" stretches into long, elegant ribbons that swirl around each other. As the ribbons touch, they fuse and glow with a soft internal light, revealing a core of vibrant emerald and electric amber. The texture is smooth and slightly damp, reflecting a soft ambient glow. The background is a void of deep charcoal, making the glowing, reshaping dough the only source of light.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  A "squishy" and melodic soundscape. The sound of air being pressed out of a soft cushion. Gentle, flute-like woodwind notes that appear only when the material glows. A seamless loop of "viscous" foley—sounds that feel thick, wet, and incredibly soft, like kneading dough in a quiet room.
  11. The Memory Foam Meltdown
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A series of interlocking, high-density memory foam blocks in sage green and buttery yellow. Deep imprints appear on their surfaces as if pressed by invisible hands, but instead of springing back, the indented areas begin to liquefy into a heavy, satin-finish polymer. This liquid "syrup" drips upward, defying gravity, and weaves into complex lattice structures. Hints of deep plum and copper foil appear within the liquid strands. The lighting is "golden hour" style, casting long, soft shadows that emphasize the changing topography of the foam.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  A "compressed" and muffled acoustic environment. The sound of slow-motion air escaping from a soft foam. A deep, resonant "thrum" that follows the gravity-defying liquid. Periodic, very soft mechanical whirs that sound like a distant, high-end camera lens focusing. The overall mood is an ultra-quiet, high-tech sanctuary.

  12. Iridescent Clay Contours
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A macro view of a solid, matte mint-green sphere and a faceted sulfur-yellow pyramid. The two shapes slowly press together; at the point of contact, they soften into a "play-dough" consistency, oozing outward in thick, scalloped ridges. As they mix, a "chemical reaction" occurs, creating veins of iridescent teal and hot pink that shimmer with a metallic luster. The shapes eventually lose their original geometry, becoming a singular, pulsing "heart" of marbled clay. The camera remains in a tight, pulsating zoom.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Tactile and earthy. The sound of thick, wet clay being worked between fingers—a "sticky" but soft foley. A low-frequency heartbeat pulse that drives the rhythm of the animation. Faint, shimmering metallic "tinkles" that coincide with the appearance of the iridescent veins.

  13. The Flocked Fluidity
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Several long, tubular shapes with a "flocked" (fuzzy) texture in chartreuse and dandelion yellow. The tubes bend and coil around each other like snakes. Where they touch, the "fuzz" melts away into a crystal-clear, jelly-like substance. Inside the clear jelly, small suspended spheres of turquoise and violet rotate slowly. The transition from the dry, fuzzy exterior to the wet, translucent interior creates a striking textural contrast. The background is a soft-focus laboratory setting.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  A contrast-based soundscape. The dry "scuff" of velvet moving against velvet, suddenly interrupted by the sound of a stone being dropped into a deep well (the "wet" sound). A constant, low-volume "underwater" bubbling that provides a sense of depth. Zero harsh frequencies; all sounds are rounded and "pillowy."

  14. Thermal Taffy Tectonics
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  Wide, flat ribbons of neon-yellow and emerald-green "taffy" being pulled and folded by invisible mechanical arms. The material is incredibly stretchy, thinning out until it becomes semi-transparent before being folded back into a thick, opaque mass. At the stress points where the taffy is thinnest, it glows with an internal white light, revealing "flecks" of charcoal and silver glitter. The movement is rhythmic and hypnotic, like a giant industrial taffy puller in slow motion.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Stretchy, elastic sounds. The high-pitched but soft "creak" of tension in a rubber band, followed by the "splat" of heavy dough hitting a surface. A rhythmic, industrial "clunk" that has been heavily muffled and softened, turning it into a soothing percussive element. A warm, analog synth pad follows the stretching movement.

  15. Opalescent Slime Synthesis
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A large, teardrop-shaped mass of "soft-touch" matte plastic in lime green suspended in mid-air. A second mass of canary-yellow "velvet liquid" rises from below to meet it. Upon merging, they don't mix but create a "lava lamp" effect inside a single, transparent membrane. The internal blobs of green and yellow dance around each other, occasionally birthing smaller droplets of bright sky blue. The outer membrane ripples like water in a breeze. The lighting is cool and clinical, with blueish highlights.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Liquid and ethereal. The sound of a large bubble moving through a thick pipe. A "glassy" resonance that rings out softly. No white noise; the background is a pure, silent void, allowing the small, wet "pops" and "clicks" of the internal droplets to be heard with crystalline clarity. A sense of floating in a weightless environment.
  16. The Rubberized Coral Bloom
  Visual Prompt (AI Video Generation)
  A macro-spectacle of forest-green and golden-yellow rubberized "coral" structures. These branch-like geometries are not rigid; they sway and pulsate as if breathing. Under a shifting light source, the "limbs" of the coral soften and begin to fuse, turning into a singular, unified sheet of matte lime-green vinyl. As the material flattens, pockets of deep indigo and magenta fluid "weep" from the pores, creating a marbleized river across the surface. The camera captures the tension as the material stretches before eventually snapping back into a soft, amorphous mound.
  Audio Prompt (AI Sound Generation)
  Organic, underwater-inspired acoustics. The sound of slow, heavy breathing muffled through a thick membrane. Soft, rubbery "thuds" and the sound of a squeegee moving over smooth glass. A deep, sub-bass "om" sound that provides a constant, meditative foundation.
  ```

- `src/common` folder should be used for common reusable features.
